etymology.description: 充值(充值) (chōngzhí, "to recharge") + 卡(卡) (kǎ, "card") forms the word 充值卡(充值卡), which refers to a card used for adding credit or value, typically to a prepaid account.
etymology.interpretation: 充值(充值) (chōngzhí, "to recharge") combines 充(充) (chōng, "fill") with 值(值) (zhí, "value") to describe the action of adding value or balance to an account. 卡(卡) (kǎ, "card") refers to a physical card, often used for storing or facilitating the transfer of this balance. Thus, a 充值卡(充值卡) is a card used specifically for recharging or topping up the balance of an account, such as a prepaid phone plan.

examples.chinese: 你需要充值卡(你需要充值卡)来加值手机话费(來加值手機話費)。
examples.pinyin: Nǐ xūyào chōngzhíkǎ lái jiāzhí shǒujī huàfèi.
examples.english: You need a recharge card to top up your mobile phone balance.

examples.chinese: 他买了一张充值卡(他買了一張充值卡)，准备给游戏账号充值(準備給遊戲賬號充值)。
examples.pinyin: Tā mǎile yī zhāng chōngzhíkǎ, zhǔnbèi gěi yóuxì zhànghào chōngzhí.
examples.english: He bought a recharge card to top up his game account.

examples.chinese: 在便利店(在便利店)，你可以很方便地购买充值卡(你可以很方便地購買充值卡)。
examples.pinyin: Zài biànlìdiàn, nǐ kěyǐ hěn fāngbiàn de gòumǎi chōngzhíkǎ.
examples.english: In the convenience store, you can easily purchase a recharge card.
